caption: Platts key facts: 12,000+ daily price assessments underpin 1,400+ exchange contracts; Brent assessment benchmarks >60% of world crude — the benchmark moat, 2014 baseline.
Capital IQ's 2014 competitive frame: 8.2B off-trading-floor market, top-10 rivals (Bloomberg, FactSet, Thomson Reuters) hold 57% — strategy of growing without displacing leaders.
Ratings revenue mix 2007-2013: structured finance collapsed 44% to 14% of revenue, corporates rose 27% to 50% — how the GFC permanently reshaped the franchise.
Ratings downside case in one slide: 2008 revenue -26% (,138M to ,583M), transaction share 49% to 30%, market issuance ,863B to ,599B — the true cyclicality bound.
The 2011-2017 journey: market cap $13B to $43B, revenue $3.8B to $6.1B, adjusted margin 30% to 47%, 26% TSR p.a. vs S&P 500's 11% — the post-breakup re-rating.
China greenfield plan: 100%-owned domestic rating agency with a China-scale rating in the world's 3rd-largest bond market — a strategic bet that vanished from every later deck.

Resilience evidence panel: US corporate ratings revenue stable vs GDP 2008-17, Platts grew while customers' revenues stagnated, MI renewals 90% to 95% — management's case for the 85% capital-return commitment.
The capital-allocation philosophy anchor: 1pp of revenue growth creates ~5x more enterprise value than 1pp of margin (HOLT analysis) — why SPGI prioritizes top-line over margin.
Merger terms: all-stock 0.2838x, IHS Markit holders get 32.25%, EV $44B at 28.2x LTM EBITDA pre-synergies (19.6x post) — the price paid for the transformation.
Original synergy program: ~$680M EBITA impact from ~$480M cost synergies plus ~$350M revenue synergies — the benchmark all later delivery claims measure against.

Pro forma 2021 division mix of the $12.4B combined company: Ratings 33.1%, MI 31.4%, Commodity Insights 13.5%, Mobility 10.1%, Indices 10.1%, Engineering 3.2% — the starting portfolio, nearly half of which is now being unwound.

MI product-line revenue by merger provenance (2021 PF $3,890M): Desktop $995M all-SPGI; Data & Advisory $1,164M 76% INFO; Enterprise Solutions $1,038M 99% INFO; Credit & Risk $693M 97% SPGI — which MI businesses are IHS-heritage.
The Peterson-era scorecard: share price +409% vs proxy peers +183% (Nov-2013 to Nov-2022), adjusted margin 33.7% to 45.3%, with the full acquisition/divestiture churn map.
Passive runway: passive share of global investible assets 14% (2008) to 36% (2021); ETF AUM expected to double by 2026 — the secular driver behind Indices' asset-linked fees.
Vitality Index defined: revenue from new/enhanced products, 8% of 2021 revenue, target >10% by 2026 — the innovation KPI later quarterly decks track (12% by 4Q25).

The 2022 adjacency bets: Private Markets $600M and Sustainability & Energy Transition $800M of 2026E revenue (~34% CAGR) — private markets roughly delivered; sustainability stalled at half the target and was rehomed.
First post-ChatGPT moat statement: data + Kensho + brand as the competitive advantages, and the AI data policy — proprietary data will not be released for third-party commercialization.
Global refinancing study: the multi-year rated-debt maturity wall chart that anchors Ratings' medium-term growth case — the format management reissues every January and July.

With Intelligence rationale: $1.8B for scaled contributory private-markets data (70k funds, 30k investors, 350k deals), high-teens growth, dilutive 2026 and accretive 2027 — the anchor asset of the private-markets push.

Cheung-era strategy triad: Advance market leadership, Expand high-growth adjacencies (private markets, energy expansion), Amplify enterprise capabilities & AI — the frame all 2026 reporting maps onto.

The moat-quantification slide: >95% of revenue from proprietary data, processes and tools — Benchmarks 63%, Proprietary & Curated Data 15%, Workflow Tools 13%, undifferentiated <5% (management-defined test).
Ratings demand runway: $8.2T of rated corporate debt matures 2026-2028, +26% vs the $6.5T average three-year wall across 2015-2024 studies — the quantified refinancing tailwind.
Enterprise data estate: 350PB+, 535 unique datasets, 10M+ data units processed by AI monthly, 400M+ companies with credit-risk insights — the scale claim behind the AI-readiness strategy.

AI investment timeline: over $1B invested since 2018, from Kensho through LLM-ready APIs, MCP, Grounding Agent and hyperscaler collaborations (Anthropic, Google, Microsoft) — the foundation of the AI-distribution thesis.

2022-2025 delivery: revenue $11.8B to $15.3B (9% CAGR), margin +180bp/yr, EPS +17% CAGR; 145% of adjusted FCF returned ($21B buybacks + $4B dividends vs $17B FCF) — returns exceeded the 85% framework via divestiture proceeds.

New 3-5yr targets: enterprise 7-9% OCC growth, 50-75bp/yr margin expansion ex-OSTTRA, double-digit EPS growth; divisions MI 6-8%, Ratings 6-9%, Energy 6-8%, Indices 10-12% — note division margin targets were dropped after the 2022 vintage misses.

2026 billed-issuance outlook: FY2025 billed issuance $4,327B (+11%) guided up low-to-mid single digits, with AI-ecosystem debt supply named as a driver — the deceleration behind Ratings' 4-7% 2026 guide.

AI commercialization KPIs: 300+ Kensho LLM-ready API customers, API calls doubled Feb-Mar 2026, AI-product buyers' ACV growing 1.3x (MI) and 2x (Energy) vs other customers — the first hard numbers on AI monetization.

MI data differentiation pyramid: 12% of MI revenue undifferentiated, 23% is redistributed Ratings content, 37% workflow tools — the divisional counterpoint to the enterprise >95%-differentiated claim.
Mobility Global at a glance: FY25 revenue $1,750M (+8.5%), 40.6% adjusted EBITDA margin, 100% of top-40 OEMs, 40K+ dealers, 53M+ Car Care users — the SpinCo's carve-out baseline.

CARFAX contributory flywheel: 38B+ records from 177K+ sources (92K service shops, 6.3K police agencies), every participant contributes data and receives value back — the network-effect moat of the spin's largest business.

Mobility 2014-2025 track record: revenue $529M to $1,750M with organic growth of 9-18% in every year except 2020's -2%, against an auto industry that was flat-to-negative most years — above-market growth through three cycles.

Mobility value-creation framework: 7.5-10% organic revenue growth, 8-11% adjusted EBITDA growth, +50bp/yr margin after standalone reset, dividends 20-25% of net income, 75%+ of FCF returned — the SpinCo underwriting targets.
